subject: TankTechnology Asterinas bring-up on Milk-V Megrez

claims.claim: Asterinas reached an interactive tty0 BusyBox shell on a physical Milk-V Megrez RISC-V board.
claims.sample_universe: One controlled RAM-only physical canary using commit 43901d2cb304b543c438614712186bc3e05bc0a1, backed by the earlier append-only board evidence sequence.
claims.metric_definition: Kernel reaches rootfs readiness; tty0 presents the BusyBox shell; an operator completes basic local interaction during the same candidate run.
claims.denominator_or_comparator: One frozen physical candidate run; QEMU is used only for shared downstream input and terminal behavior, not as a substitute for the Megrez hardware result.
claims.safe_wording: Brought Asterinas up on a Milk-V Megrez RISC-V board to an interactive tty0 BusyBox shell.

claims.claim: The physical run reused the U-Boot framebuffer and accepted basic input through the Megrez DWC3/xHCI, onboard VIA hub, and one boot-time-connected USB HID Boot Protocol keyboard.
claims.sample_universe: The same controlled physical canary plus QEMU and Linux UHID/evdev oracle tests for shared keyboard and TTY behavior.
claims.metric_definition: Framebuffer metadata passes the RAM-only canary gate; the local shell is visible; the operator confirms character, modifier, Enter, single Backspace, navigation, and Ctrl-C behavior.
claims.denominator_or_comparator: One physical keyboard and one physical canary. The serial log proves boot and recovery but cannot record tty0 keystrokes or screen output.
claims.safe_wording: The controlled integration reused the firmware framebuffer and demonstrated basic local USB-keyboard interaction.

claims.claim: The controlled canary recovered to a fresh OpenSBI/U-Boot cycle and prompt after 180 seconds.
claims.metric_definition: The raw serial stream contains the new firmware epoch and final U-Boot prompt after the Asterinas candidate epoch.
claims.denominator_or_comparator: One run, with a single candidate booti and no persistent U-Boot environment write.
claims.safe_wording: The RAM-only canary completed its controlled recovery back to U-Boot.

claims.claim: This is the first-ever boot of Asterinas on a RISC-V development board or on Milk-V Megrez.
claims.sample_universe: Public web search and the current upstream Asterinas tree.
claims.metric_definition: A priority claim would require a comprehensive historical comparison and a definition of boot success.
claims.denominator_or_comparator: No complete comparison universe is available. Asterinas already documents RISC-V and SiFive HiFive Unleashed support.
claims.safe_wording: A new Megrez bring-up milestone for our Asterinas RISC-V work.

claims.claim: Milk-V Megrez is an officially supported Asterinas board.
claims.sample_universe: Upstream Asterinas documentation and the local development branch.
claims.metric_definition: Official support requires upstream acceptance, maintained documentation, test coverage, and a declared support tier or board contract.
claims.denominator_or_comparator: The local branch is 333 commits ahead of upstream/main and its origin is not publicly accessible without credentials.
claims.safe_wording: A controlled integration result on a development branch; not official Megrez support.
